mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations. A 1-month decline in FEDFUNDS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days through mechanical repricing of equity risk premiums.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.55% as of 2020-01-15 reflects recent Fed easing cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive FEDFUNDS changes fail to predict negative 20-day returns in > 70% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals robust labor market strength and consumer income growth, which sustains corporate earnings and equity valuations. A month-over-month increase in PAYEMS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 152,186k as of 2020-01-15 reflects sustained employment growth
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with forward equity returns (Spearman rho < 0.10 over 3-year window), or negative 20-day returns occur in > 55% of months with positive PAYEMS growth.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) measure consumer spending momentum, which drives corporate revenue and profit expectations. When RSAFS growth decelerates below its 3-month trend, equity investors reduce earnings forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: RSAFS deceleration fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) captures real economic capacity utilization and manufacturing health. When INDPRO declines month-over-month, it signals supply-side weakness and margin compression, predicting negative equity returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings downward.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes fail to predict negative 5-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) combined with declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer purchasing power and corporate earnings. This joint deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through both demand and margin channels.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month AND PAYEMS declines month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% and PAYEMS at 152,186k as of 2020-01-15
failure_condition: Joint UNRATE rise and PAYEMS decline fail to predict negative 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly measure consumer demand momentum across housing and consumption. When both decline simultaneously, it signals broad-based demand weakness that compresses equity valuations. This interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month change < 0 AND RSAFS month-over-month change < 0
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,608k and RSAFS at 529,766M as of 2020-01-15
failure_condition: Simultaneous HOUST and RSAFS declines fail to predict negative 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL combined with low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) creates a negative real interest rate environment that encourages equity risk-taking and asset price inflation. This regime predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when real rates are compressed.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 258.82 and FEDFUNDS at 1.55% as of 2020-01-15
failure_condition: Negative real rate regime fails to predict positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of economic demand. When quarterly GDPC1 growth decelerates below 2% annualized, it signals late-cycle weakness that equity investors interpret as earnings recession risk, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19,219.77B as of 2020-10-15 (latest available quarterly data)
failure_condition: Sub-2% GDPC1 growth fails to predict negative 60-day returns in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.
